summary: Backend smoke + functional tests on the new /api/sportsdb/* endpoints. Endpoints, shape, normalisation, sorting, cache-headers, and existing-endpoint smoke checks (sports/find, tmdb/search, img-proxy) all pass. However, repeated refresh=1 calls to /api/sportsdb/fixtures cause TheSportsDB free-tier 429 rate-limits, AND the backend then caches the empty 0-event payload for 30 minutes — effectively breaking the Sports Guide for everyone. Same poisoning behaviour exists in /api/sportsdb/league-season (6h TTL).

critical.issue: Cache poisoning under rate-limit. _fetch() silently swallows 429 Too Many Requests and returns None. After the per-refresh fan-out (~175 outbound requests = 35 leagues × 3 endpoints + 5 days × 14 sport filters) triggers throttling on TheSportsDB free key (123), the function still calls _cache_set(payload, 30*60) with events=[] and bySport={}. Subsequent normal GETs return cached=True with 0 events for the next 30 minutes. Reproduced live: GET /api/sportsdb/fixtures?refresh=1 returned 0 events for 3 consecutive calls; current cache holds cached=true events=0.

Fan-out volume blows past TheSportsDB free-key budget. The function issues ~175 requests in parallel per cold load (35 leagues for eventsnextleague + 35 for eventsseason + 5*15 daily fan-out). Backend logs show consecutive 429 Too Many Requests responses for nearly every URL. Spec requires >=100 events; observed 121 on first ever cold call, then 53 on a follow-up refresh, then 0 once rate-limited. Currently fails the >=100 / >=8 sports SLO non-deterministically.

minor.issue: _fetch() logs upstream errors at DEBUG level only and never distinguishes 429 from generic failure. No visibility for ops; suggest WARN-level on non-200 + propagate a soft-error counter into payload (e.g. upstream_errors:int).
Same cache-poisoning shape — TTL is 6 hours, so an unlucky cold call during throttling can hide EPL fixtures for 6 hours. Currently observed cached=true events=[] for league_id=4328.

Duplicate league id 4391 used for both 'NFL' (American Football) and 'WTA Tour' (Tennis) in TOP_LEAGUES. byLeague aggregation will collide if both produce events. Tennis WTA id should be 4392 or similar — please verify with TheSportsDB.

action_items: Fix cache-poisoning: DO NOT call _cache_set when the aggregated events list is empty (or fall back to keeping the previous cached value).
Cap upstream concurrency (e.g. asyncio.Semaphore(8)) and back off on 429 to avoid hammering TheSportsDB. Consider exponential retry + short circuit if a 429 streak is detected.
Reduce fan-out: per the agent-to-agent note, the cache is meant to be sticky; consider lazy-loading season fetches per-league only when /league-season is explicitly hit.
Promote 429/non-200 logging from DEBUG to WARN and expose an `upstream_errors` counter in the /api/sportsdb/fixtures response for diagnostics.
Fix duplicate id 4391 (NFL vs WTA Tour) in TOP_LEAGUES in /app/backend/sportsdb.py line ~53/84.
Optional: serve stale-while-revalidate — return previously cached payload while a background task refreshes.
critical_code_review_comments: sportsdb.py line ~129 (_fetch): swallows all non-200s into None — no retry, no log severity, 429 indistinguishable from connection error.
sportsdb.py line ~320 (_cache_set after fan-out): writes payload unconditionally, even when events=[]. Must guard `if events: _cache_set(...)` or fall back to existing cache.
sportsdb.py line ~84: idLeague 4391 reused for two sports (NFL + WTA Tour); first one to register a real league wins, the other shows up as empty.

sportsdb.py: each /api/sportsdb/fixtures cold call issues ~175 outbound HTTP requests with no concurrency cap. Under the free key (123) this is virtually guaranteed to trip rate-limits — both for our pod and any other tenant sharing the same upstream key.

success_rate.backend: 85% (6 of 7 pytest tests pass; the failing one — fixtures volume — fails because of the cache-poisoning + rate-limit bug above, not a test bug. Pre-rate-limit the endpoint did return 121 events across 12 sports.)

seed_data_creation: None — endpoints are read-only against external TheSportsDB API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cache may currently be poisoned with cached=true events=0 (30 min TTL on fixtures, 6h TTL on league-season). Either wait for TTL or restart backend to clear in-memory cache before retesting. When testing volume (>=100 events), make a SINGLE cold call (after a long pause or after backend restart) — repeated refresh=1 calls will all 429 and look like the backend is broken. backend_test file: /app/backend/tests/test_sportsdb.py. Tests use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env.
rca of the issue: Root cause: The free TheSportsDB API key '123' is heavily rate-limited and the backend fan-out pattern (35 curated leagues × eventsnextleague + 35 × eventsseason + 5 days × ~14 sport filters via eventsday = ~175 outbound requests per cold load) trips the rate-limit almost immediately. _fetch() then silently swallows the 429s and returns None for all calls, and the resulting empty payload is written to the cache with full TTL. Reproduction: (1) GET /api/sportsdb/fixtures?refresh=1 three times in succession; events count drops 121 → 53 → 0. (2) GET /api/sportsdb/fixtures — returns cached=true, events=0 for next 30 min. Mitigation: (a) Don't cache empty results: `if events: _cache_set(...)` else keep prior cache. (b) Add asyncio.Semaphore(8) and retry-with-backoff inside _fetch on 429. (c) Reduce fan-out — make eventsseason lazy via /league-season only. (d) Log 429s at WARN level. (e) Fix duplicate league id 4391.
